events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario is about ladder reinforcement spacing being exceeded in block/brick laying on a commercial project. This is a non-compliance issue. Ladder reinforcement (joint reinforcement) spacing exceeding requirements means the work doesn't meet code/spec.

Decision: fail
Risk: medium or high - excessive spacing of ladder reinforcement reduces structural integrity of masonry walls
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: maybe 80-90% since rough-complete
Confidence: high
Findings: ladder reinforcement spacing exceeded
Actions: rework to correct spacing, install additional reinforcement
References: TMS 402/602

  "findings": [
    "Ladder reinforcement spacing exceeded the maximum required interval, reducing horizontal joint reinforcement coverage in the masonry wall",
    "Non-compliant joint reinforcement spacing may compromise flexural and shrinkage crack control per TMS 402/602"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop further masonry closure in affected areas until spacing is verified and corrected",
    "Rework affected courses to install ladder reinforcement at the specified spacing interval",
    "Document actual spacing measurements and submit corrective action plan to third-party inspector"
  ],
  "rationale": "Ladder (joint) reinforcement spacing exceeding the specified or code-required maximum interval is a non-compliance under TMS 402/602, which governs placement and spacing of horizontal joint reinforcement for crack control and structural performance. Excessive spacing reduces the intended reinforcement coverage and must be corrected before closure.",
  "references": [
    "TMS 402/602 — Building Code Requirements and Specification for Masonry Structures (joint reinforcement spacing and placement)"
  ]
}
